name: Production-Ready Bash Script Template
description: Create a reusable bash script template with strict error handling, argument parsing, logging, cleanup traps, and help text.
trendingReason: Well-structured bash scripts prevent outages caused by silent failures in CI/CD pipelines

steps.title: Create the script file with a shebang
steps.description: Start with the bash shebang and strict mode flags to catch errors early.
steps.command:
cat > script.sh << 'SCRIPTEOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
IFS=$'\n\t'
SCRIPTEOF
steps.tip: Use #!/usr/bin/env bash instead of #!/bin/bash for portability across systems where bash may not be in /bin.

steps.title: Add script metadata and constants
steps.description: Define the script name, version, and directory for reliable relative paths.
steps.command: echo 'readonly SCRIPT_NAME="$(basename "$0")"' >> script.sh && echo 'readonly SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"' >> script.sh && echo 'readonly VERSION="1.0.0"' >> script.sh

steps.title: Add a usage function
steps.description: Create a help function that documents all options and arguments.
steps.command:
cat >> script.sh << 'USAGEEOF'

usage() {
  cat << EOF
Usage: ${SCRIPT_NAME} [OPTIONS] <argument>

Options:
  -h, --help      Show this help message
  -v, --verbose   Enable verbose output
  -d, --dry-run   Show what would be done without executing
  --version       Show version

Examples:
  ${SCRIPT_NAME} -v /path/to/target
  ${SCRIPT_NAME} --dry-run /path/to/target
EOF
  exit 0
}
USAGEEOF

steps.title: Add a cleanup trap
steps.description: Register a cleanup function that runs on exit, error, or interrupt to remove temp files.
steps.command:
cat >> script.sh << 'TRAPEOF'

cleanup() {
  local exit_code=$?
  # Remove temp files, restore state, etc.
  rm -f "${TMPFILE:-}" 2>/dev/null || true
  exit "$exit_code"
}
trap cleanup EXIT ERR INT TERM
TRAPEOF
steps.warning: Always quote variable expansions in cleanup to avoid word splitting on filenames with spaces.

steps.title: Add a logging function
steps.description: Create colored log functions for consistent output formatting.

steps.title: Make the script executable and test it
steps.description: Set execute permissions and verify the script runs without errors.

faq.question: What does set -euo pipefail do?
faq.answer: -e exits on any error, -u treats unset variables as errors, -o pipefail catches errors in piped commands. Together they prevent silent failures that cause data loss or incorrect behavior.

faq.question: Why use IFS=$'\n\t' at the top?
faq.answer: The default IFS includes spaces, which causes word splitting on filenames with spaces. Setting IFS to only newline and tab prevents this class of bugs when iterating over file lists or command output.

faq.question: Should I use bash or POSIX sh for scripts?
faq.answer: Use bash for scripts that run on known systems (CI, servers) where bash is available. Use POSIX sh (#!/bin/sh) only for maximum portability across minimal environments like Alpine containers or embedded systems.
